text:
Market today: The mood is cautious for tech and chips. Headlines point to "imported volatility" hitting chip stocks and general negative sentiment around major tech players like SpaceX and AI leaders. This directly impacts your concentrated portfolio.

Your holdings: SOXL (-34.51%, SELL) and SPAL (-34.54%, NEW) are your most pressing concerns due to their leveraged nature, decay, and significant drawdowns. SMH (-7.43%) is also feeling the chip sector pain. Margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on further news impacting the chip sector, especially how the "imported volatility" from SK Hynix plays out. Also, monitor the broader tech sentiment given the SpaceX and AI news.

Bottom line: Aggressive risk management on your leveraged, deeply red positions is critical with current tech/chip headwinds.
plays: SOXL: Your leveraged chip bull is down 34.51% and flagged "SELL". Review your stop at 200; consider trimming to manage risk given the chip sector news.
SPAL: This new leveraged ETF is also down significantly (-34.54%). Re-evaluate your stop at 21.38 and position size to control downside.
No new buys today - hold and watch. The tech/chip sector has headwinds, and no compelling new long setups align with a strong catalyst.
